scenario: Keystone Payroll renders 7000 payslip previews per month inside Camunda 8.8 Forms. A summary template begins with the line Hello {{username}}, you are {{if isAdmin then "an admin" else "a user"}}, and the team must understand what can appear between the double curly braces so 200 admins per day see the badge within 5 seconds.
question: What can be placed inside the double curly braces in Feelers templating syntax for an insert?

options.text: Any valid FEEL expression whose evaluated result is then inserted at that position in the rendered template by the Feelers runtime on each form render
Only a single bare variable name from the form data, with no operators or conditionals allowed inside the double curly braces of an insert
Only a string literal enclosed in double quotes, with all dynamic content moved to surrounding loop or conditional blocks of the Feelers template
Any JavaScript expression that the browser evaluates client-side when the form is rendered, with full access to the window object of the host page

a.text: Correct. To insert a variable use double curly braces, and any valid FEEL expression can be used within these double braces, including conditionals like if-then-else.

b.text: Incorrect. Inserts accept any valid FEEL expression, not only a bare variable name; operators and conditionals work between the braces.

c.text: Incorrect. Inserts evaluate FEEL expressions, not just string literals; dynamic content does not have to be moved out of the braces.

d.text: Incorrect. Feelers evaluates FEEL inside the braces, not JavaScript, and templates do not run with browser window access at render time.

explanation: Feelers inserts use double curly braces and may contain any valid FEEL expression. The runtime evaluates the FEEL inside the braces and substitutes its result into the rendered output.
